text:
Market today: Risk appetite is healthy - tech led H1 gains, massive AI fund flows (MGX $49B) keep the narrative intact, and export control relief on Anthropic models signals Washington is loosening the leash on AI. That's the real driver for your chip/tech book today.

Your holdings: Everything is green and above stops - no fires. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF; decay and margin together mean a sharp reversal hits you hard - watch it closely. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile; leveraged, fast-moving, margin amplifies any drop. QQQ (+3.04%) and SMH (+3.07%) are fine but both sit in premium - not the time to add size.

Watch: (1) NVDA price action - it's the bellwether for everything you own; a breakdown below $197 would pressure SMH and SOXL fast. (2) Any Iran/oil escalation headline - oil spike can rotate money out of tech quickly.

Bottom line: Book is clean, let winners run, but keep a tight eye on your leveraged names SOXL and SPAL - margin turns a bad day into a bad week.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200 - that's wide on a 3x. If you're on margin, consider tightening to $230-240 range to protect the +4.8% gain against a fast reversal.
NVDA scanner shows entry $198.39-198.62, stop $197.35 - grade C setup (mixed timeframes), R only 1.4. Weak conviction; only nibble tiny if it holds $198.39 cleanly, no margin.
No new buys today beyond that - existing book is working, no need to chase into premium.
